title: Transitions in development - an interview with Natalie Dye.
abstract: Natalie Dye is group leader at the Mechanobiology Institute (MBI), Singapore. She is interested in using the Drosophila wing and human organoid systems to understand how cell dynamics are coordinated in time and space to build complex 3D tissues. In this interview, we chatted to Natalie about her research interests, her path to becoming a group leader, and the importance of finding a supportive network of peers and mentors.
